_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Federal-House batch: resolve TX placeholders/vacant + UT-04. vacant-tx-23->Brandon Herrera (R nominee, seat vacant after Gonzales resigned 4/2026 & withdrew post-primary). tx-38-r-placeholder->Jon Bonck (R nominee, Hunt ran for Senate, Bonck won 5/26 runoff). tx-37-d-placeholder DELETED via dedup-merge into greg-casar (Casar is the real TX-37 D nominee; moved from TX-35 under redrawn map). carlos-quintanilla LOST TX-33 primary (14%, 3rd). jonny-larsen still running UT-04 open seat but thin policy footprint -> roster_verified, unscored to avoid a misleading lone-cell 100%.

set.name: Brandon Herrera
set.office: U.S. Representative TX-23 (2026 R nominee · seat vacant — Gonzales resigned 4/2026 & withdrew post-primary; Herrera is nominee, faces D Katy Padilla Stout 11/3)

profile.background: Brandon Herrera — firearms entrepreneur/YouTuber ('The AK Guy'); 2026 Republican nominee for TX-23 (open after Rep. Tony Gonzales resigned April 2026 and withdrew from the primary, leaving Herrera — the #2 March-3 finisher — as nominee). Faces Democrat Katy Padilla Stout in November (a special election to fill the term remainder is pending).
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Scored from his campaign Issues page (HARD). Pro-life and 2A planks are explicit; marriage/LGBTQ, election-integrity, and specific border-barrier/deportation positions are not stated on the Issues page — left null (no inference from party).

vacant-tx-23.notes_set: Repointed from VACANT placeholder to the actual 2026 GOP nominee Brandon Herrera. Gonzales (R) resigned April 2026; Herrera and Gonzales were the top-two March-3 finishers, Gonzales then withdrew, making Herrera the nominee. NOTE for a later pass: TX-23 records terri-leo-wilson (R) and royce-west (D) appear to be defeated primary candidates and likely need status:lost.

0.note: Campaign Issues page: 'defending life at every stage'; pledges to oppose government funding for abortion and sponsor the Infant Born Alive Act.

1.note: Issues page: block Red Flag laws and new firearms restrictions; 'strong defender of the Second Amendment.'

set.name: Jon Bonck
set.office: U.S. Representative TX-38 (2026 R nominee · open seat — Hunt ran for U.S. Senate; Bonck won 5/26 runoff)

profile.background: Jon Bonck — Houston mortgage loan officer and Second Baptist Church deacon; 2026 Republican nominee for TX-38 (open after Rep. Wesley Hunt vacated to run for U.S. Senate). Led a 12-candidate March-3 primary (~47%) and won the May 26 runoff over Shelly deZevallos. Endorsed by Trump, Cruz, Scalise, Mike Johnson, and SBA Pro-Life America.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Scored from campaign-site planks (HARD) + SBA Pro-Life endorsement. Marriage/LGBTQ and election-integrity not stated — null. Foreign-policy and 2A cells scored from his own America-first / 'no permission needed' wording.

tx-38-r-placeholder.notes_set: Repointed from TX-38 R placeholder to the actual 2026 GOP nominee Jon Bonck, confirmed via the May 26, 2026 primary runoff. Wesley Hunt vacated TX-38 to run for U.S. Senate (lost that primary).

0.note: Campaign site: 'Life begins at conception. No compromise.' SBA Pro-Life America endorsed him 5/15/2026 ('100 percent pro-life').
Campaign site: 'Finish the wall. End the invasion. Put Texas first.' — completed physical border barrier.
Campaign site on 2A: 'God-given rights don't need D.C.'s permission' — opposes permit requirements (constitutional carry).

1.note: Campaign site: 'No more blank checks for foreign wars. Put America first.' — opposes endless foreign-war funding.

0.note: Campaign site: 'No mandates. No coercion. No mRNA vaccines.' — opposes pharmaceutical/vaccine mandates.

set.office: U.S. Representative TX-33 (2026 D candidate — LOST 3/3 primary, ~14%, 3rd place; Allred/Johnson advanced to runoff)

profile.background: Carlos Quintanilla — longtime Dallas immigrant-rights activist; 2026 Democratic candidate for TX-33. Finished 3rd (~14%) in the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary behind Colin Allred (44%) and Rep. Julie Johnson (33%) — did not advance.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — LOST the primary. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: a pro-life, religious-liberty Democrat (iVoterGuide candidate survey). Scored on his cited self-statements; pro-immigration (path-to-citizenship) stance scored contrary on border. Guns/election/economy/foreign — no citable position, null.

carlos-quintanilla.notes_set: Candidacy-currency fix: LOST the March 3, 2026 TX-33 Democratic primary (~14%, 3rd). Status set to lost. Pro-life + religious-liberty Democrat per his iVoterGuide survey (cross-party deviation).

0.note: iVoterGuide survey: 'Human life begins at conception and deserves legal protection'; supports the Born Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act.
iVoterGuide survey: 'Religious liberty ... deserves the highest level of protection'; pledges to protect Christians sharing the Gospel.

1.note: Supports religious-liberty/conscience exemptions for those who believe marriage is the union of one man and one woman.
Longtime immigrant-rights activist; supports comprehensive reform with a path to citizenship — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

set.office: U.S. Representative UT-04 (2026 D candidate · open seat — Owens not seeking reelection; June 23 primary)

profile.background: Jonny (Jonathan) Larsen — USMC infantry combat veteran (two Iraq tours, 2003-2004); healthcare-systems administrator; 2026 Democratic candidate for UT-04 (open seat after Rep. Burgess Owens announced 3/4/2026 he will not seek reelection). On the June 23, 2026 Democratic primary ballot per his notarized Utah Declaration of Candidacy.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Active candidate CONFIRMED (FEC committee active; Utah Declaration of Candidacy filed 3/9/2026; open seat confirmed). His campaign site is a JS-only SPA with no crawlable Issues text and social accounts were bot-blocked, so the only citable policy statement found is a war-powers/Article-I objection to the U.S.-Israel strike on Iran. Left UNSCORED (roster_verified) rather than publish a misleading lone-cell 100% for a progressive. Re-score when an Issues page / questionnaire becomes available.

jonny-larsen.notes_set: Candidacy-currency verify: still running; UT-04 is open (Owens not seeking reelection, announced 3/4/2026). Insufficient citable policy footprint to score — roster_verified, unscored. Documented (non-rubric) position: opposed the U.S.-Israel strike on Iran as an unconstitutional war 'without the approval of Congress.'

set.office: U.S. Representative TX-35 (sitting · 2026 D nominee for TX-37 — won 3/3 primary 80.75%; Doggett retired)

greg-casar.notes_append: De-duplicated 2026-06-17 — merged tx-37-d-placeholder. Casar is the actual TX-37 Democratic nominee: he won the March 3, 2026 TX-37 primary with 80.75% (no runoff). Under the 2026 redrawn Texas map he moved from TX-35 to TX-37; Lloyd Doggett retired rather than face him.
